title: The River the Titan Remembered
tagline: In EP026, the Titan mentioned a river in its third message. The team couldn't find any river at those coordinates. Today they decide to look.
hook_line: In EP026, when the Titan listed things from before it went underground, it mentioned a river. There is no river at those coordinates. There has not been a river there in any record, map, or memory anyone on Olympus can find. The Titan described it with specific detail: cold, came from the north, changed direction once each season. The team decides to find out what happened to it.

scenes.title: The Third Message, Revisited
scenes.narration: Athena had the Titan's third message from EP026 on a new scroll — just the list. Temperatures, rock types, creature names, the taste of the water at a specific depth. And in the middle of the list: 'THE RIVER THAT CAME FROM THE NORTH AND TURNED ONCE IN SPRING.' Athena had drawn a map. She had cross-referenced every river, stream, and water channel in the Olympus Sea region, going back through every record she had. Nothing. No river had ever been recorded at the coordinates implied by the Titan's other geographical references. 'It's describing something that doesn't exist anymore,' she told Poseidon that morning. Poseidon read the message. 'Or something we've never found.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Athena's desk with the Titan's third message and a hand-drawn map — the coordinates marked, the surrounding geography detailed — the gap where the river should be, clearly empty on every map she's drawn — Archie on the desk looking at the map — Poseidon reading the message, specifically: 'THE RIVER THAT CAME FROM THE NORTH AND TURNED ONCE IN SPRING' — his note has a sardine on it — the maps spread around them, multiple versions, all showing the same absence — morning light and the specific quality of a puzzle that has existed for a while but is only now being examined — #4DB6E8 and map-scroll tan, the absence on the map the key visual

scenes.title: Perseus Knows Terrain
scenes.narration: Perseus had been on more eastern valley expeditions than anyone. He had walked the terrain around the Olympus Sea from the east. He looked at Athena's map for a long time. 'Here,' he said. He pointed to a dry channel — not marked as a former river, just an unusual depression in the terrain. 'This runs north-south with one east bend. I thought it was just a valley feature.' Athena marked it. 'When did you find this?' 'Last season,' he said. 'I was looking for a good view of the sea.' He had been looking east, toward where Medusa lived. Athena looked at him. She marked the channel.
scenes.visual_prompt: Perseus and Athena over the map — Perseus identifying the dry channel, pointing specifically — 'I thought it was just a valley feature' — Athena marking it on the map — the channel aligning with the Titan's description: north-south with one eastern bend — the quiet implication of why Perseus was out there: looking east, toward Medusa — Athena registering this, marking the channel, not commenting on the direction — Archie watching Perseus look at the map, the same understanding in Archie's eyes that was in his eyes in EP024 — the map with the new marking, the dry channel now a hypothesis — #FFD700 and map-scroll warmth, the mark on the map the turning point

scenes.title: Hercules's Question
scenes.narration: Hercules had been listening. He asked: 'If the river was there when the Titan was awake, and it's not there now, what happened to it?' 'It was redirected,' Athena said. 'Or it dried up.' 'Or,' Hercules said, and he said the next part carefully, 'or the Titan moved, and it moved the ground.' Everyone looked at him. 'The Titan is large,' Hercules added. 'I am stating that it is large.' 'Yes,' Zeus said. 'We are aware.' 'If the Titan moved in its sleep,' Hercules said, 'the river could have been blocked. The ground underneath it could have shifted. The channel could have redirected underground.' Athena looked at her map. 'That would mean the river still exists,' she said. 'Just underground.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Hercules's careful question: 'what happened to it?' — his hypothesis: the Titan moved in its sleep, shifted the ground, blocked or redirected the river — Hercules adding 'I am stating that it is large' in his earnest way — Zeus's 'we are aware' — the team's collective pause as Athena follows the logic: if the ground shifted, the river could be underground now — Athena's 'that would mean the river still exists. Just underground.' — Poseidon's expression changing, because if there's a river underground, he would know — everyone looking at Poseidon — Poseidon looking at his notes, which have a sardine on them — #FFD700 and the warmth of a good idea landing

scenes.title: Poseidon Goes to Look
scenes.narration: Poseidon went to the dry channel. He brought his most formal note-taking equipment — three scrolls and the good quill. He stood at the north end of the channel and listened the way he listened to water. He stood there for a while. Then he knelt and put both hands on the ground. He stayed like that for longer. Then he looked up and said, to no one in particular: 'There is water moving underneath this. It is cold. It comes from the north.' He paused. 'And it turns east in spring.' He sat down on the ground. He wrote it in his scroll. He added a sardine to the note out of habit. He looked at the dry channel above the underground river that the Titan had remembered for however long it had been asleep.
scenes.visual_prompt: Poseidon at the dry channel — formal note-taking equipment, three scrolls, the good quill — standing at the north end listening to the ground in the way he listens to water — kneeling, hands on the ground — the long stillness — and then: 'there is water moving underneath this. It is cold. It comes from the north.' — and then the pause — 'and it turns east in spring.' — Poseidon sitting down on the dry ground above the underground river — writing in the scroll, adding a sardine out of habit — looking at the channel — the underground river visible as a faint blue glow in cross-section beneath him — the Titan correct, 3,000 years later, the river still there — #4DB6E8 and earth tones, the underground glow the key visual

scenes.title: What to Tell the Titan
scenes.narration: The team assembled to write the response. Zeus asked: 'Do we tell it that the river is still there?' 'Yes,' Athena said immediately. 'It described a river. We found it. It is underground now, but it still exists. It still turns east in spring. We should tell it.' Zeus nodded. His crown was straight. 'Do we tell it why the river went underground?' Pause. 'We think,' Athena said carefully, 'because it shifted in its sleep. We believe the ground moved under the river's channel.' Zeus's crown shifted. Then straightened. 'Yes,' he said. 'We tell it.' Perseus said: 'It should know it didn't lose it. The river remembered the same direction.' Everyone looked at him. 'That is a good sentence,' Athena said.
scenes.visual_prompt: The team around the draft response — Zeus's question: 'do we tell it?' — Athena: 'yes. It described a river. We found it. It still exists.' — the disclosure about the why: the ground shifted under its sleep — Zeus's crown: shifts left at the weight of telling the Titan it may have redirected a river while sleeping, then straightens as he decides — Perseus's contribution: 'it should know it didn't lose it. The river remembered the same direction.' — Athena: 'that is a good sentence' — Perseus slightly surprised to have said the right thing — Archie watching Perseus — the draft response forming on Athena's scroll — #FFD700 and the warmth of good collaboration, Zeus crown detail clear

scenes.title: The Titan's Reply
scenes.narration: Poseidon sent the message via the dolphins. The Titan's response came back within the hour. The dolphins translated it in segments. The first segment: 'THE RIVER. YES. I KNEW IT WAS THERE. I FELT IT CHANGE.' Second segment: 'I DID NOT KNOW I HAD MOVED. I AM SORRY ABOUT THE RIVER.' Third segment — Poseidon read this one to the team slowly: 'THANK YOU FOR FINDING IT. I HAVE BEEN THINKING ABOUT THAT RIVER FOR A LONG TIME. I DID NOT KNOW IF IT WAS STILL THERE.' Zeus's crown was straight for 4 seconds. Then he said: 'Write back. Tell it the river is well. The river is still doing what it does. The river is fine.' And then, after a pause: 'We are glad it asked about the river.'
scenes.visual_prompt: The three-part dolphin reply delivered in sequence: 'THE RIVER. YES. I KNEW IT WAS THERE. I FELT IT CHANGE.' / 'I DID NOT KNOW I HAD MOVED. I AM SORRY ABOUT THE RIVER.' / 'THANK YOU FOR FINDING IT. I HAVE BEEN THINKING ABOUT THAT RIVER FOR A LONG TIME.' — the team listening to Poseidon read each segment — the Titan apologizing for accidentally redirecting a river while sleeping, and thanking them for finding it — Zeus's crown straight for 4 seconds — Zeus's response instructions: 'tell it the river is well. The river is fine. We are glad it asked about the river.' — the weight of the Titan having worried about this river during however long it was underground — #4DB6E8 and warm gold, the dolphin relay visible in the foreground

scenes.title: Archie at the Channel
scenes.narration: That evening, Archie flew to the dry channel. He landed at the north end — where Poseidon had stood. He stood there in the way Archie stood at things: perfectly still, not looking at the surface but at whatever was underneath it. The underground river made no sound that anything above ground could hear. But Archie stood there for a while. Then he hopped one step east. Then back. He was tracing the turn — the point where the river changed direction each spring, underground now but still turning, still following the same path. He flew back toward Olympus at dark. Whatever he had confirmed, he kept to himself.
scenes.visual_prompt: Archie at the dry channel alone in the evening — landing at the north end, Poseidon's precise location — standing in his Archie stillness, looking at what's underneath rather than what's above — the underground river as a faint blue glow cross-section visible beneath him — Archie hopping one step east, back — tracing the spring turn — the specific quality of Archie doing what Archie does: not collecting data, confirming something he already suspected — the evening sky, Olympus lit in the background — Archie flying back at dark, his silhouette against the evening — whatever he understood, private — #1A1A4E and evening gold, the underground river glow the key visual under Archie

viral_hook: The Titan mentioned a river in its message from EP026. There was no river at those coordinates in any record. Poseidon went to the dry channel, put his hands on the ground, and found it — underground, still turning east in spring, exactly where the Titan remembered it. The Titan replied: 'I have been thinking about that river for a long time. I did not know if it was still there.'
highlight_note: The Titan's apology: 'I DID NOT KNOW I HAD MOVED. I AM SORRY ABOUT THE RIVER.' It accidentally redirected a river in its sleep and has been worried about it ever since. Zeus says: 'Tell it the river is fine. We are glad it asked.'
short_title: The Titan Was Worried About the River This Whole Time
rewatch_bait: Scene 2 — Perseus identifying the dry channel. He was looking east, toward where Medusa lives. Athena marks it, doesn't comment on the direction. EP024 established Perseus visiting the east path at sunset. He's been out there often enough to know the terrain.
youtube_title: The Titan mentioned a river that doesn't exist. Poseidon put his hands on the ground and found it — underground. | Little Olympus S2 EP9
lesson: Things that seem lost are sometimes just redirected. The Titan moved in its sleep and accidentally changed where the river went. But the river didn't disappear — it kept going, just underground. Perseus's description of this: 'It should know it didn't lose it. The river remembered the same direction.'
arc_notes: Titan arc major development: the Titan has specific, accurate geographical memories that predate written records. It moved in its sleep and unknowingly redirected a river — and has been worried about it. The team's discovery and disclosure deepens the relationship. Zeus crown: straight 4 seconds for the Titan's 'I have been thinking about that river.' Archie tag: tracing the river's spring turn, confirming something he already knew. Perseus's 'the river remembered the same direction' is the seasonal rewatch quote for this episode.
